title: Keep service boundaries cohesive
description: A service owns its data and exposes intent-level operations; callers do not reach across into another service's storage.
body: Use when adding an operation or deciding where logic lives. Do not let one service read or write another service's tables directly or leak internal models across the boundary. Actions: keep each write behind the owning service's operation, expose intent (placeOrder) not storage (insertRow), and version the contract. Validation signals include a check that no cross-service direct DB access exists and that the boundary is exercised by an integration test against the public operation. Risk boundary: shared-table coupling across services blocks independent deploys and is a structural finding to flag for review.
